chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Requires Full Blood Money and Half Blood Money in Cases Other Than the Taking of a Life

text: 5292 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ كُسِرَ بُعْصُوصُهُ فَلَمْ يَمْلِكِ اِسْتَهُ مَا فِيهِ مِنَ اَلدِّيَةِ فَقَالَ "اَلدِّيَةُ كَامِلَةً" قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ وَقَعَ بِجَارِيَةٍ فَأَفْضَاهَا وَ هِيَ إِذَا نَزَلَتْ بِتِلْكَ اَلْمَنْزِلَةِ لَمْ تَلِدْ فَقَالَ "اَلدِّيَةُ كَامِلَةً".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5292 - Hisham ibn Salim narrated from Sulayman ibn Khalid who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man whose coccyx (tailbone) was broken, resulting in his inability to control his bowels.
Imam (as) replied: "The full blood money (diya) is due."
Sulayman further asked Imam (as) about a man who engaged in intercourse with a slave girl and caused ifda' (a severe injury of female reproductive organs), and she, in that condition, would never be able to bear children.
Imam (as) replied: "The full blood money (diya) is due."
